name: AutoWIFI
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``AutoWIFI`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: sudo autowifi  (interactive TUI); also `sudo autowifi scan -i wlan0mon -d 30`, `sudo autowifi capture -i wlan0mon -b <BSSID> -c 6 -t 120`, `sudo autowifi crack handshake.cap -w rockyou.txt [--backend hashcat]`

examples.command: sudo autowifi  (interactive TUI); also `sudo autowifi scan -i wlan0mon -d 30`, `sudo autowifi capture -i wlan0mon -b <BSSID> -c 6 -t 120`, `sudo autowifi crack handshake.cap -w rockyou.txt [--backend hashcat]`

algorithms: airodump-ng subprocess scan with signal + client tracking
WPA 4-way handshake capture via aireplay-ng deauth + airodump-ng
WPS pixie-dust and PIN brute force via reaver/bully
WEP ARP-replay / fragmentation / chopchop via aireplay-ng
cracking dispatch to aircrack-ng / hashcat / john
MCP server exposing 12 tools (scan_networks, capture_handshake, capture_pmkid, wps_pixie_dust, deauth, crack_handshake, etc.) to AI agents
produces: JSON/HTML/text reports of discovered networks (BSSID, ESSID, encryption, signal, WPS, clients)
novelty: none — thin wrapper. All recon and attack logic is subprocess calls to airodump-ng/aireplay-ng/hcxdumptool/reaver/bully/hashcat; deps.py only probes for those binaries. The only marginally non-baseline element is an MCP server interface so AI coding assistants can drive the same wrapped tools — an integration surface, not a new recon algorithm.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
